content:
Hello and welcome! I am currently a postdoctoral research fellow in Statistics under the supervision of Professor Alberto Roverato at the Department of Statistical Sciences, University of Padova, Italy. Our objective is to conduct a research activity on the development of new statistical tools for learning the structure of complex networks characterized by high dimensionality in non-standard experimental setups.
During the three-year experience of the Ph.D. program in Padova, I have grown very fond of the school, the faculty and the research activities. In particular, it is a pleasure and excitement for me to work under the enthusiastic and dedicated guidance of Professor Alberto Roverato. My doctoral research focuses on the problem of joint learning of multiple graphical models. The full description for those interested is available in my attached curriculum vitae.
My research interests are generally in statistical learning and applied statistics. Specifically, I would apply machine learning methodologies as well as statistical modeling to analyze and interpret large, complex data sets, especially data related to biology and genetics.
I am a motivated, young researcher on the path to building the scientific world of my own. It will certainly be an honor for me to participate in any collaborative relationship.
summary: Hello and welcome! I am currently a postdoctoral research fellow in Statistics under the supervision of Professor Alberto Roverato at the Department of Statistical Sciences, University of Padova, Italy. Our objective is to conduct a research activity on the development of new statistical tools for learning the structure of complex networks characterized by high dimensionality in non-standard experimental setups.
title: Dung Ngoc Nguyen

summary: The activity is part of the AFOSR project and as a specific object the identification of a network, based on a set of data in the event that the reference context presents a natural symmetrical structure. Specifically, the network can be seen as the union of two blocks, each vertex in the first block has a homologous vertex within the second block, and two blocks are interconnected. This problem can be seen as a special case of a colored graphical model in which the model space forms a lattice structure. The problem of model selection through greedy procedures therefore requires the development of algorithms that allow the search space to be efficiently explored through local steps.
tags: Algorithms for graphical models with symmetries
title: Algorithms for graphical models with symmetries
